beliefs.gloss: Visual common sense is learnable from training data
beliefs.evidence: prediction from Xinfer becomes progressively more accurate... adapting to task-specific transformations through few-shot test-time training.

beliefs.gloss: Classical vision backbones can address ARC effectively
beliefs.evidence: all U-Net variants achieve decent accuracy, suggesting that this problem can also be effectively addressed by classical vision backbones.

beliefs.gloss: Explicit 2D positional modeling is essential
beliefs.evidence: Extending from 1D positional embedding to its 2D counterpart is beneficial.

beliefs.gloss: Explicit 2D positional modeling is essential
beliefs.evidence: This is observed in both (b) absolute and (c) relative positional embeddings.

beliefs.gloss: Explicit 2D positional modeling is essential
beliefs.evidence: we replace the 2D ROPE... with a 1D ROPE and observe a degradation of 3.5 points.

beliefs.gloss: Canvas patchification enriches data space, reduces overfitting
beliefs.evidence: the 2×2 patchification leads to a noticeable gain... each patch can cover multiple colors... enriches the data space.

beliefs.gloss: Canvas patchification enriches data space, reduces overfitting
beliefs.evidence: each patch can cover multiple colors (as in natural images), which substantially enriches the data space for learning.

beliefs.gloss: Canvas enables geometric augmentations for invariance
beliefs.evidence: we apply fully flexible translation augmentation on the canvas... yields an additional gain.

beliefs.gloss: Augmentations foster invariance to geometric transformations
beliefs.evidence: highly capable network architectures still benefit greatly from translation and scale augmentations. We draw similar observations.

beliefs.gloss: ViT incorporates locality and translation invariance biases
beliefs.evidence: Unlike translation invariance, which can be partially addressed by patchification.

beliefs.gloss: Canvas enables geometric augmentations for invariance
beliefs.evidence: we further ap- ply the scale augmentation enabled by the concept of can- vas. Scale augmentation yields a substantial gain.

beliefs.gloss: Augmentations foster invariance to geometric transformations
beliefs.evidence: highly capable network architectures still benefit greatly from translation and scale augmentations. We draw similar observations.

beliefs.gloss: Scale augmentation yields substantial gain due to ViT bias
beliefs.evidence: ViT architecture has little to no inductive bias about scale invariance. This can explain why scale augmentation yields a substantial gain.

beliefs.gloss: Scale augmentation yields substantial gain due to ViT bias
beliefs.evidence: the ViT architecture has little to no inductive bias about scale invariance.

beliefs.gloss: Scale augmentation yields substantial gain due to ViT bias
beliefs.evidence: This can explain why scale augmen- tation yields a substantial gain.

beliefs.gloss: Larger models in current setting lead to overfitting
beliefs.evidence: Going beyond this regime can lead to overfitting in our current setting, as shown in Tab. 1.

beliefs.gloss: Visual common sense is learnable from training data
beliefs.evidence: offline training greatly improves the per- formance of TTT, suggesting that common sense about the visual world can be learned.

beliefs.gloss: Some ARC tasks are solvable from scratch
beliefs.evidence: even without offline training, our TTT strategy can achieve nontrivial accuracy (26.4), suggesting that some tasks... can be solved tabula rasa.

beliefs.gloss: Multiple test tasks cannot be assumed available simultaneously
beliefs.evidence: performing TTT independently for each test task yields substantially better performance... relies on a stronger assumption about the availability of multiple test tasks.

beliefs.gloss: Single pixel error invalidates entire ARC prediction
beliefs.evidence: a mistake on even a single pixel renders the entire prediction incorrect. This may explain the large gain seen here.
